title: SSH Key Setup: Generate, Configure & Use SSH Keys
description: Think your server is secure? 80% of developers mess up this one crucial step. Master SSH key generation and server setup in under 5 minutes.

schemas.headline: SSH Key Setup: Generate, Configure & Use SSH Keys (Step by Step)
schemas.description: Think your server is secure? 80% of developers mess up this one crucial step. Master SSH key generation and server setup in under 5 minutes.

mainEntity.name: How to set up ssh keys?

acceptedAnswer.text: To set up SSH keys, open your terminal and run the command `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"`. Press Enter to save the key to the default location, and enter a strong passphrase. Then, use `ssh-copy-id user@hostname` to add the key to your server.

mainEntity.name: What is the best algorithm for ssh key generation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The recommended algorithm for SSH key generation is Ed25519. It provides better security and performance compared to older algorithms like RSA, while producing much shorter and easier-to-manage keys.

mainEntity.name: Is there a simple ssh keygen tutorial for beginners?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, our ssh keygen tutorial covers Windows, Mac, and Linux. On all three platforms, you can open a terminal (Command Prompt, Terminal app, or bash shell) and use the built-in `ssh-keygen` command to generate your key pairs seamlessly.

mainEntity.name: How do I add ssh key to server securely?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can add an ssh key to a server automatically using `ssh-copy-id username@remote_host`. Alternatively, you can manually append the contents of your public key (`~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub`) to the server's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` file.

mainEntity.name: How do I configure SSH for GitHub or GitLab?

acceptedAnswer.text: After generating your SSH key, copy the public key contents using `cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub`. Log into GitHub/GitLab, go to SSH and GPG keys in settings, and paste the key. Test the connection with `ssh -T git@github.com`.
